mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of periods when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, recession risk pricing accelerates, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors de-risk portfolios.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through earnings revision cycles.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in over 55% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days specifically during disinflationary periods.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually, or the mechanism's predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.01) outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Higher total nonfarm payrolls directly increase aggregate wage income and consumer spending capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns (p > 0.10) across a 10-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 45% of high-growth months.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 265, persistent inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 265, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing GDP growth and rising retail sales indicates inventory overhang and unsustainable consumption, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in over 70% of cases, or the interaction effect is fully explained by lagged market momentum rather than macro fundamentals.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production during periods of stable unemployment signals healthy capacity utilization without labor market overheating, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth yields negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations when UNRATE is stable, or the signal's Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5 in walk-forward testing.
